analogy: Think of "Environment" through the chapter's own opening images: After the long vacation, when Ravi started going to school again, he noticed that the only playground next to his school was dug up. People said that a huge building with many flats will be constructed there. Ravi was almost in tears, when he realised... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Environment is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that after the long vacation, when Ravi started going to school again, he noticed that the only playground next to his school was dug up.

The text explains that people said that a huge building with many flats will be constructed there.

The text explains that ravi was almost in tears, when he realised that the big playground with its soft grass, marigolds and butterflies is gone for ever.

The text explains that in the assembly, the Principal too sadly observed, “See how our environment is changing.” In the class Ravi asked his teacher, “What is environment?” “Whatever you see in your surroundings,” said the teacher.

The text explains that ravi thought aloud, “That means, the school building, tables, chairs in the classroom, even that open field, the road, the garbage, my friends – all are parts of our environment”! “Yes” said the teacher, “but wait…..

The text explains that some objects are created by nature – for example, mountains, rivers, trees, animals.

The text explains that others are made by people – for example roads, cars, clothes, books”.

The text explains that make a list with your classmate sitting next to you, of the creations of nature and by human beings.

The text explains that environment is our basic life support system.

The text explains that it provides the air we breath, the water we drink, the food we eat and the land where we live.

The text explains that how do human beings modify this natural environment?

The text explains that the car fumes pollute the air, water is collected in a pot, food is served in vessels and land is used to build factories.

The text explains that human beings make cars, mills, factories and manufacture containers.

The text explains that this is how human beings modify natural environment.

The text explains that 1 Environment Ravi, Paramjeet, Jessy, Mustafa, Asha were all excited about making the list. “Why is our environment changing?” asked Iqbal. “It’s all because of our needs.

The text explains that 2 OUR ENVIRONMENT Natural Human made Human made Human Components of Environment Water (Hydrosphere) Air (Atmosphere) Land (Lithosphere) Living Things (Biosphere) Parks Buildings Bridges Roads Industries Monuments Individual Family Community Religion Educational Economic Political Situation Fig.

The text explains that lithosphere is the solid crust or the hard top layer of the earth.

The text explains that it is made up of rocks and minerals and covered by a thin layer of soil.

The text explains that it comprises various sources of water and different types of water bodies like rivers, lakes, seas, oceans, etc.

The text explains that (iii) Which are the major components of the environment?

The text explains that (iv) Give four examples of human made environment.

The text explains that (vi) Which are the two major components of biotic environment?

The text explains that (a) Desert (b) Aquarium (c) Forest (ii) Which is not a component of human environment?

The text explains that (a) Land (b) Religion (c) Community (iii) Which is a human made environment?

The text explains that (a) Mountain (b) Sea (c) Road (iv) Which is a threat to environment?

The text explains that (a) Growing plant (b) Growing population (c) Growing crops 3.

The text explains that (i) Biosphere (a) blanket of air which surrounds the earth (ii) Atmosphere (b) domain of water (iii) Hydrosphere (c) gravitational force of the earth (iv) Environment (d) our surroundings (e) narrow zone where land water and air interact (f) relation between the organisms and their surroundings 4.

The text explains that (i) Man modifies his environment (ii) Plants and animals depend on each other 5.

The text explains that imagine an ideal environment where you would love to live.

Source: NCERT Class 7 Our Environment, Chapter 1: Environment

prelims_traps: Do not treat 1 Environment and 2 OUR ENVIRONMENT as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Environment; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Environment through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 1 Environment and 2 OUR ENVIRONMENT to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.

It is a combination of natural and human made phenomena.
